what_it_does: Records a completed request (Heart H1.2) to add reverse graph traversal and a unified relations query surface to cc_board.py, including implementation notes, state history, and proof-by-use validation.
observations: Item type is request and state is done.
Author session is ch-8djrpmsl with an authored_by link to the same session.
Builds on prior work H1.1 (commit 68c7eda) and was completed in commit 70e0e8a.
reverse_traverse finds all board items with a link whose target equals a given opaque target string, matching without address resolution.
relations(addr, direction in/out/both, kind) provides a unified query over incoming and outgoing edges.
Hydration is optional and performed via resolve_address, not during the structural match.
Fail-loud behavior on unregistered link kind.
Proof-by-use validated against real genesis items, including the promoted_from chain item-15aaf9da -> item-e523b30d.
References exclusive fork access and a next-ranked clone (clone-cacc9e8b).
No code symbols are present; the file is narrative frontmatter only.
standouts: Reverse traversal matches the OPAQUE target string projection-over-rows rather than resolving addresses.
Unified relations surface combines in/out/both directions using the existing edge registry and resolve_address for hydration.
Proof-by-use on real genesis data and fail-loud kind validation are explicit quality mechanisms.
Clear separation between structural edge query and optional address hydration.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, created/updated timestamps, and history transitions.
Follows the noticeboard item format for tracking requests through state changes.
Records rationale, dependencies, commit references, and validation notes in free-text fields.
Links the authoring session via an authored_by relation.
concerns: Heavily dependent on external context (H1.1, cc_board.py, Heart program, clone/fork conventions) not present in the file.
Dense jargon and shorthand may obscure meaning for readers outside the project.
No code symbols or API signatures are included, so implementation details must be inferred from prose.
notes: A done request ticket documenting the design and validation of reverse graph traversal and unified relations queries in cc_board.py.

purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it documents H1.2 reverse/query traversal for cc_board.py and records it as completed, which is exactly what the content shows.
apparent_themes: Incremental feature building on prior commits
Separation of structural matching from address hydration
Reuse of existing registry rather than parallel engines
intention_signals: To make the board queryable from both directions by adding incoming-edge traversal and a single relations API, while keeping matching structural and hydration optional.
novelty: The explicit split between opaque-target structural matching for reverse_traverse and optional resolve_address hydration, plus a unified in/out/both relations surface instead of separate forward and reverse engines.
contribution: A completed design record for reverse_traverse and relations, capturing the rationale for opaque-string matching, fail-loud kind handling, optional hydration, and real-board proof-by-use validation.
summary_for_embedding: A done noticeboard request item that documents the implementation of reverse graph traversal and a unified in/out/both relations query surface in cc_board.py, building on H1.1 and validated against real genesis board data.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate the completion of H1.2, which adds backward-edge and bidirectional relation querying to the board memory layer.
